etymology.description: 木(木) (mù, "wood") + 倪(倪) (ní, "limit") = phono-semantic composition with 倪 providing the sound and 木 referencing the wooden structure of a loom
etymology.interpretation: The character 檷 incorporates 木(木) (mù, "wood") to suggest a connection to wooden structures, such as a loom, with 倪(倪) (ní, "limit") serving as a phonetic component that hints at the pronunciation of the character.
